concept: Net / Mesh / To Catch in Network
description: A mesh of cord or thread for catching or containing; to catch with such mesh. 'Mera' names what catches through openness—the paradox of holes that hold.
sound_symbolism: The word meshes wide. 'Me' connects in pattern, knots meeting. Then 'ra' reaches outward, net spreading to catch.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'lo mera' (nets) - for fishing, catching, containing through mesh. It works through 'roe': 'mia roe mera shalu howela.' (I take fish with the net.) Related: niro (web), phaliso (network).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Interconnection as net—Indra's net where each node reflects all others, everything connected.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Nets sustained communities—fishing, gathering, the ancient technology of cooperative catching.
